title: Cooperative Calligraphy

jokes.text: Three hours longer than it took Duncan to think up this assignment.
jokes.setup: Question about papier-mache drying time
jokes.punchline: Comparison to teacher's lazy assignment creation
jokes.explanation: Sharp, economical dig at the teacher with good structure. Jeff's sarcasm comes through clearly.

jokes.text: I still think that man is going to evolve into woman, not dragon monster with three legs.
jokes.setup: Discussion about evolutionary chart project
jokes.punchline: Reveals someone created a bizarre three-legged dragon monster as evolution's endpoint
jokes.explanation: Absurd visual revealed through dialogue - perfectly fits Community's weird sensibility. Good character moment for Britta's pseudo-intellectualism.

jokes.text: Or should I say a catch to date. Oof, hope you just come up with that.
jokes.setup: Jeff makes a wordplay about having 'a date to catch'
jokes.punchline: Britta's immediate cringe reaction to the bad wordplay
jokes.explanation: Standard wordplay/groan combo. Britta's reaction saves it from being completely hackneyed.

jokes.text: Look out! Drive-by deaning!
jokes.setup: Dean enters the scene unexpectedly
jokes.punchline: Plays on 'drive-by shooting' with his own name
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dean character moment - his tendency to make everything about himself with wordplay. High character integration and economy.

jokes.text: I expect all of you to lend a paw.
jokes.setup: Dean wants students to help at puppy parade
jokes.punchline: Uses 'paw' instead of 'hand' to match the puppy theme
jokes.explanation: Predictable pun but fits Dean's character. Low originality but serviceable.

jokes.text: Except you, Jeffrey. I know you've gotta catch to date. Oh, like you're famous for your wit.
jokes.setup: Dean references Jeff's earlier bad wordplay
jokes.punchline: Jeff's sarcastic response about the Dean's own wit
jokes.explanation: Good callback with Jeff's characteristic sarcasm. Shows their relationship dynamic well.

jokes.text: Want to see if those wiener dogs are born that way, or if they start off normal and then get wiener.
jokes.setup: Discussion about going to puppy parade
jokes.punchline: Troy's innocent but bizarre theory about dachshund development
jokes.explanation: Perfect Troy moment - childlike curiosity leading to absurd logic. Highly quotable and character-specific. Using 'get wiener' as a verb is brilliant.

jokes.text: More relatable?
jokes.setup: Abed mentions he uses mechanical pencils now
jokes.punchline: Annie questions if this choice is for relatability reasons
jokes.explanation: Sharp meta joke about Abed's character awareness. Perfect economy - maximum meaning in two words.

jokes.text: I don't want people think of me as a handicap. If anything, this chair makes me more than a human.
jokes.setup: Annie offers to push Pierce's wheelchair to parade
jokes.punchline: Pierce's delusional claim that disability makes him superhuman
jokes.explanation: Classic Pierce - taking something negative and spinning it into grandiose self-aggrandizement. Dark but true to character.

jokes.text: You move it by blowing into this, uh, tube here. It's the most expensive one. I outbid three hospitals for this baby, and it was worth every penny.
jokes.setup: Pierce explaining his high-tech wheelchair
jokes.punchline: Admits he outbid hospitals for a wheelchair, showing his priorities
jokes.explanation: Perfect Pierce moment - wealthy, selfish, and missing the point. The image of him bidding against hospitals is darkly funny.

jokes.text: We passed 'sorry, Annie' eight pens ago!
jokes.setup: Group casually apologizes for missing pen
jokes.punchline: Annie reveals this is a recurring problem with specific count
jokes.explanation: Great escalation reveal and perfect Annie obsessiveness. The specific count makes it funnier and more believable.

jokes.text: Accidents don't just happen over and over and over again, okay? This isn't budget daycare.
jokes.setup: Someone suggests the pen was taken accidentally
jokes.punchline: Annie's increasingly frantic denial with daycare comparison
jokes.explanation: Good character escalation. The daycare comparison shows Annie's growing desperation and need for control.

jokes.text: Okay, whoever insidiously and with great malice aforethought abducted Annie's pen, confess, repent, and relinquish so we can leave.
jokes.setup: Jeff tries to mediate the pen situation
jokes.punchline: Uses overly formal legal language for a missing pen
jokes.explanation: Classic Jeff - using his lawyer background to make a simple situation overly formal. Good character integration.

jokes.text: Sometimes I think I lost something really important to me, and it turns out I already ate it.
jokes.setup: Discussion about whether pen was accidentally taken
jokes.punchline: Troy's bizarre personal anecdote about eating lost items
jokes.explanation: Perfect Troy absurdism. The matter-of-fact delivery of something completely nonsensical is brilliant character work.

jokes.text: It's not a pen! It's a principle.
jokes.setup: Jeff dismisses it as just a pen
jokes.punchline: Annie's dramatic escalation to principle
jokes.explanation: Good escalation moment. Annie's tendency to make everything about bigger issues. Great economy and quotable.

jokes.text: Because this is starting to feel like a bottle episode.
jokes.setup: Group stuck in room looking for pen
jokes.punchline: Abed recognizes they're in a TV bottle episode
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta joke that breaks the fourth wall while staying in character. This IS a bottle episode and Abed knows it. Perfect Community moment.

jokes.text: Hey, meatball, did you take Annie's pen to make life more like Benny hill or whatever you do?
jokes.setup: Jeff addressing Abed about the situation
jokes.punchline: Dismissive comparison to Benny Hill with vague 'whatever you do'
jokes.explanation: Jeff's dismissive attitude toward Abed's meta awareness. 'Meatball' is a good character touch but overall fairly standard.

jokes.text: I wouldn't do that. I hate bottle episodes. They're wall-to-wall facial expression and emotional nuance. I might as well sit in the corner with a bucket on head.
jokes.setup: Abed denies taking pen to create bottle episode
jokes.punchline: Explains his hatred of bottle episodes in meta terms while being in one
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta commentary where Abed explains why he hates the very episode format they're currently in. The bucket imagery is perfect absurdist touch.

jokes.text: Oh, it all starts with a quick look-see into someone's bag and then it's a brisk peeka-roony at our phone records, and before you can say '1984,' the thought police are forcy-worcing you to bend and spread.
jokes.setup: Annie wants to search Britta's bag
jokes.punchline: Britta's absurd escalation to dystopian surveillance state with made-up words
jokes.explanation: Perfect Britta moment - pseudo-intellectual activism with made-up cutesy words. 'Forcy-worcing' is brilliant character-specific language.

jokes.text: Are the thought police gonna make love to us? Do they find thoughts in our butts? I knew I should have read that book.
jokes.setup: Britta's reference to 1984 and 'bend and spread'
jokes.punchline: Troy's innocent misunderstanding of both the book and the implications
jokes.explanation: Classic Troy innocence meeting adult themes. The logical confusion about where thoughts are stored is perfectly childlike.

jokes.text: Welcome to the gross business of martial law. Welcome to what used to be individuality, seized and disintegrated by cowardly groupthink. Welcome, my friends! Welcome to the machine!
jokes.setup: Britta's bag gets searched revealing condoms
jokes.punchline: Dramatic speech about fascism over a pen search
jokes.explanation: Pure Britta - turning minor inconvenience into political oppression. The Pink Floyd reference is perfect for her character.

jokes.text: Well, Annie, it looks like you were wrong. Britta does come prepared for one thing. Or six.
jokes.setup: Condoms fall out of Britta's bag after she claimed not to be prepared
jokes.punchline: Jeff counts the condoms and makes the obvious joke
jokes.explanation: Fairly obvious sex joke but delivered with Jeff's characteristic smarm. The count makes it slightly better.

jokes.text: The Patriot Act cuts both ways. Actually, it's pretty one-sided. That's kind of the point.
jokes.setup: Britta tries to make political point about surveillance
jokes.punchline: Jeff's correction that the Patriot Act is intentionally one-sided
jokes.explanation: Smart correction of Britta's misused political rhetoric. Shows Jeff's actual intelligence when he cares.

jokes.text: Yes, we were all just thinking that in 1856.
jokes.setup: Pierce suggests Troy stole the pen based on statistics
jokes.punchline: Troy calls out the racist implication with historical reference
jokes.explanation: Smart response to Pierce's racism. Troy showing intelligence while calling out historical context. Great economy.

jokes.text: What if a ghost took the pen?
jokes.setup: Tense accusation scene
jokes.punchline: Troy's innocent suggestion of supernatural explanation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Troy logic - when adult problems get complicated, suggest something childlike. This becomes a running theme. Maximum economy.

jokes.text: Side effects: Verbal dysphasia and octopus loss. I don't see anything on this squirrel about memory, Troy.
jokes.setup: Troy suggests Pierce forgot due to painkillers
jokes.punchline: Pierce misreading medication label with nonsensical side effects
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist moment. 'Octopus loss' as a side effect and calling the bottle a 'squirrel' shows Pierce's drug-addled state perfectly.

jokes.text: Pierce, you have something to tell us? Yes. Is it me, or has it become really obvious that Jeff took the pen
jokes.setup: Jeff thinks Pierce is about to confess
jokes.punchline: Pierce instead accuses Jeff
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection moment. Pierce's obliviousness to social cues while pointing fingers is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Lockdown! Abed, seal the doors. Nobody leaves until this pen shows up.
jokes.setup: Group about to leave without finding pen
jokes.punchline: Annie goes full authoritarian over a pen
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of Annie's control issues. The dramatic language for such a minor issue is great character work.

jokes.text: Well, tell your disappointment to suck it. I'm doing a bottle episode!
jokes.setup: Jeff canceling his date due to lockdown
jokes.punchline: Jeff embracing the meta reality of being in a bottle episode
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta moment where Jeff breaks character awareness. The enthusiasm for being in a TV trope is perfect Community humor.

jokes.text: You just became my hero.
jokes.setup: Jeff fully embracing the bottle episode
jokes.punchline: Abed's approval of Jeff's meta awareness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed response - appreciating someone else's meta awareness. Great economy and character moment.

jokes.text: Oh my God. Are you charting our menstrual cycles?
jokes.setup: Discovery of Abed's mysterious charts
jokes.punchline: Revelation of extremely personal tracking
jokes.explanation: Brilliant cringe moment that's both shocking and perfectly in character for Abed. The violation of privacy for 'efficiency' is darkly funny.

jokes.text: Abed just became my hero. Can I have a little... No!
jokes.setup: Revelation about menstrual cycle tracking
jokes.punchline: Pierce's inappropriate appreciation cut off by Annie
jokes.explanation: Typical Pierce inappropriate response. The cutoff makes it funnier than letting him finish the creepy thought.

jokes.text: Well, well, well, Harvey Keitel. Well, what do you know, Henry David Thoreau. My oh my, Mike Tyson
jokes.setup: Shirley refusing to open her bag
jokes.punchline: Jeff's nonsensical string of rhyming celebrity names
jokes.explanation: Weird Jeff moment showing his tendency to perform. The random celebrity rhyming is absurd but fits his character.

jokes.text: People like you are the reason we took so long to get into Vietnam.
jokes.setup: Pierce acts decisively by dumping Shirley's bag
jokes.punchline: Completely backwards understanding of Vietnam War criticism
jokes.explanation: Perfect Pierce moment - completely missing the historical consensus on Vietnam while being confidently wrong. Pure character gold.

jokes.text: Nice try, Stephen Fry.
jokes.setup: Jeff trying to leave the locked room
jokes.punchline: Random British intellectual reference as insult
jokes.explanation: Weaker Jeff moment. The reference doesn't quite land and feels more random than clever.

jokes.text: It's a pen now? Really? It's not a principle anymore now it's a pen? Why the change of heart?
jokes.setup: Jeff calls it 'just a pen' after earlier escalation
jokes.punchline: Annie throws his words back at him with escalating intensity
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Annie's escalating paranoia. The repetition and emphasis build tension effectively.

jokes.text: You precocious little bitch!
jokes.setup: Annie's increasingly aggressive accusations
jokes.punchline: Jeff finally snaps with contradictory insult
jokes.explanation: 'Precocious little bitch' is a perfect contradiction - combines childish and adult insults. Shows Jeff's frustration boiling over.

jokes.text: What are those underwear made out of? They look luxurious. Oh. They're an organic soy-cotton blend.
jokes.setup: Everyone in underwear searching for pen
jokes.punchline: Troy's innocent appreciation of fabric quality in awkward situation
jokes.explanation: Good Troy moment of finding wonder in mundane details even during crisis. The specific fabric blend is a nice Abed touch.

jokes.text: Don't you usually wear the stripey turquoise beetlejuice numbers? What does she mean 'usually'?
jokes.setup: Annie comments on Jeff's underwear choice
jokes.punchline: Implies Annie has seen Jeff's underwear before, causing awkwardness
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment that implies uncomfortable history. 'Beetlejuice numbers' is a funny specific description.

jokes.text: This is how super villains are created.
jokes.setup: Pierce strapped to chair getting his cast cut
jokes.punchline: Abed's genre-aware observation about their actions
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed meta commentary. He recognizes they're creating a villain origin story while it happens. Great economy and awareness.

jokes.text: It smells like a Waffle House sink!
jokes.setup: Pierce's cast being cut open
jokes.punchline: Specific, disgusting comparison to restaurant hygiene
jokes.explanation: Vivid, gross imagery that's very specific. 'Waffle House sink' is a perfect gross-out reference that feels authentic.

jokes.text: Have we not gotten to a place free of judgment yet?
jokes.setup: Everyone reacting with disgust to Pierce's cast contents
jokes.punchline: Pierce's plea for acceptance while being genuinely disgusting
jokes.explanation: Good Pierce moment of false victimhood while being genuinely gross. The irony of asking for non-judgment works well.

jokes.text: What's more likely? That someone in this group doesn't belong in this group? Or ghosts?
jokes.setup: Group torn apart by suspicion
jokes.punchline: Jeff seriously considers ghost theory as more plausible than betrayal
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback where Jeff's logical mind breaks down and Troy's absurd suggestion becomes preferable to facing group dysfunction.

jokes.text: So I see it as a lot like the movie paranormal activity, except for more boring and fancy.
jokes.setup: Troy explaining his ghost theory
jokes.punchline: Compares their situation to horror movie but 'more boring and fancy'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Troy description - reducing horror movie to 'boring and fancy.' Shows his unique perspective on everything.

jokes.text: Something you and your puppies could only dream of you non-miraculous son of a bitch.
jokes.setup: Dean asks what they did in the room
jokes.punchline: Jeff's over-the-top response treating their dysfunction as achievement
jokes.explanation: 'Non-miraculous son of a bitch' is a great Jeff insult. Shows his tendency to make everything sound more important than it is.

tmdb_overview: Annie's missing pen leads to friction within the group.
insight: Bottle episode delivers 45 jokes in 45 minutes with escalating character chaos.
